title: In transit
summary: Sydney before the airport. A run to loosen the body, coffee in hand, and that suspended hour of watching people move through departures.

blocks.caption: The morning started in Sydney. Legs awake, coffee warm, departure boards glowing.

blocks.text: I ran before the airport because I needed to move. Just enough to wake the legs and quiet the noise a little. Then coffee, a seat, a book open but not really held for long. I kept looking up, wondering where everyone was going.

blocks.story: Sydney felt clear and slightly unreal in that pre-departure way. The body had already started the day before the flight did. Feet on pavement. Breath working. Then the stillness of the terminal, where everything narrows into waiting. I sat there with coffee and read in fragments, more interested in faces, bags, departures, the pull of elsewhere. Excited, yes. But also aware of how thin the line is between ordinary routine and leaving.

details.label: What I ate
details.value: Coffee before the airport, then whatever felt simple enough to travel with.

details.label: Distance ridden
details.value: No riding yet. Just a run before departures.

details.label: Map pins
details.value: Sydney. Airport. The next point not visible yet.

blocks.caption: The air changes first. Then the light. Then the small sense that something has opened.

blocks.text: By the end of the day I am carrying the same body into a different place. Not settled. Not clear. But pointed somewhere.
